question: In Python programming, what is the best way to think about a 'class'?

options.A: A real robot that can move around
options.B: A blueprint or template plan used to build things
options.C: A list that only holds numbers
options.D: An error message that stops your program

question: What do we call a real, individual object that is built from a class blueprint?

Look at this code snippet:

class Robot:
    pass

Which line of code correctly builds a new object from this Robot class blueprint?
What does the Python keyword 'pass' mean when you use it inside a new class definition?

options.A: It means the code should move to the next file
options.B: It acts as a placeholder that means 'nothing here yet' so the program doesn't crash
options.C: It sends an answer back to the main program
options.D: It prints a success message on the screen

question: If you define one class called 'DogRobot', how many robot objects can you create from that single blueprint?

options.C: As many objects as you want
